title: Exocharts Pricing Guide 2026: All Plans, Costs & Hidden Fees
meta_title: Exocharts Pricing Guide 2026: Plans, Costs & Fees Explained
meta_description: Exocharts pricing guide 2026: Standard $29/mo, annual $290/yr. No free tier. Compare with TradingView, ATAS, Sierra Chart. See all costs & hidden fees.
excerpt: Get the 2026 Exocharts pricing guide: all plans, real costs, hidden fees, and how it compares to TradingView, ATAS, and Sierra Chart. No fluff—just facts.
content:
Exocharts Overview 2026: What Makes It Stand Out?

Exocharts has carved a niche for itself among crypto order flow traders, futures scalpers, and volume profile analysts. With a 3.7/5 rating, it’s recognized for its focused order flow toolset, clean footprint chart implementation, and the ability to cover both futures and crypto markets from a single platform. Unlike broader charting solutions, Exocharts is purpose-built for those who need advanced order flow and footprint charting, making it a top choice for traders who demand clarity and precision in their analysis.

However, it’s important to note that Exocharts does not offer a free tier—users must pay to access any features. Its feature set is also more limited compared to all-in-one platforms, focusing primarily on core order flow tools. For a deeper dive into its strengths and weaknesses, check out our Exocharts review.

Exocharts Pricing Plans 2026: All Tiers & Features

As of 2026, Exocharts offers a straightforward pricing structure designed to cater to both individual traders and professional desks. Here’s a breakdown of the current plans:

  • Standard Plan: $29/month — Includes all core order flow and footprint charting tools, access to both crypto and futures markets, and multiple visualization modes. This is the most popular plan for active traders.
  • Annual Plan: $290/year — Offers the same features as the Standard Plan but at a discounted rate (equivalent to $24.17/month). Ideal for committed users looking to save over the long term.
  • Enterprise/Desk Plan: Custom pricing — Tailored for trading firms or desks needing multiple seats, API access, or custom integrations. Pricing is available upon request.

There is no free trial or free tier, which means you must commit financially to try the platform. This is a key consideration for new users. For more details on how Exocharts stacks up against other platforms, see our comparison.

Hidden Fees and Extra Costs: What to Watch For

While Exocharts’ pricing appears transparent, there are a few potential extra costs to be aware of:

  • Data Fees: Exocharts includes crypto and some futures data, but certain futures exchanges may require separate data subscriptions, especially for professional use. These can range from $10 to $50/month depending on the exchange.
  • API Access: Advanced users or trading desks needing API access for automation or custom integrations may face additional charges (typically negotiated for enterprise plans).
  • Account Upgrades: If you need more simultaneous chart windows or advanced visualization features, these may only be available on higher-tier or custom plans.

Always confirm with Exocharts support if your intended use case might incur extra costs, especially if you trade on less common exchanges or require advanced integrations.

Quick Comparison: Exocharts vs. Leading Charting Platforms (2026)

PlatformRatingPrice (Monthly)Best ForFree Tier?Key Features
Exocharts3.7/5$29Order flow, footprint chartsNoCrypto & futures, multiple footprint modes
TradingView4.6/5$14.95Multi-asset charting, scriptingYesStocks, crypto, indicators, Pine Script
ATAS4.2/5$69Advanced order flow, futuresNoFootprint, cluster analysis, DOM
Sierra Chart4.0/5$26Professional futures tradingNoCustom studies, advanced charting

This table highlights that while Exocharts is competitively priced for its niche, it lacks a free tier and has a narrower feature set compared to broader platforms like TradingView. For more details, see our TradingView review and ATAS review.

Who Should Choose Exocharts?

Exocharts is best suited for:

  • Crypto Order Flow Traders: Those who need precise footprint charts and order flow visualization for crypto markets.
  • Futures Scalpers: Traders who rely on tick-level data and volume profiles for fast-paced decision-making.
  • Volume Profile Analysts: Users who want multiple footprint visualization modes and clean, distraction-free charting.

If you require multi-asset charting, scripting, or a broader feature set, platforms like TradingView or Sierra Chart may be a better fit. For a head-to-head breakdown, visit our Exocharts vs. TradingView comparison.

Exocharts Pricing FAQ 2026

  • Is there a free trial for Exocharts in 2026? No, Exocharts does not offer a free trial or free tier. All users must pay to access the platform.
  • Are there discounts for annual billing? Yes, the annual plan is $290/year, saving you about 17% compared to monthly billing.
  • What about data fees? Crypto data is included, but some futures exchanges may require separate subscriptions.
  • Can I cancel anytime? Yes, subscriptions can be canceled at any time, but there are no refunds for unused time.
  • Is Exocharts suitable for beginners? It’s best for traders who already understand order flow concepts. Beginners may find the learning curve steep.

Final Recommendation: Is Exocharts Worth It in 2026?

If you’re a serious crypto or futures order flow trader who values clean footprint charts and focused analysis tools, Exocharts remains a strong contender in 2026. Its $29/month price is fair for the niche features it offers, though the lack of a free tier and limited feature set beyond order flow tools may be a drawback for some. For those needing broader charting, scripting, or multi-asset support, consider alternatives like TradingView or ATAS. Always factor in potential data fees and check if your preferred exchanges are supported before subscribing.

For a detailed breakdown of Exocharts’ pros, cons, and user experiences, read our full Exocharts review.
